title: TLS, SSE-S3, SSE-KMS, Bucket Keys, and Encryption Boundaries · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent team receives different tenants, file sizes, media types, regions, compliance duties, traffic, failures, and cost limits and transfers tls, sse-s3, sse-kms, bucket keys, and encryption boundaries into a new encryption and key-authorization contract. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of encryption and key-authorization contract with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal d…
objectives: Separate TLS in transit, S3 server-side encryption, KMS envelope encryption, and application client-side encryption by threat and feature tradeoff.
Choose SSE-S3, SSE-KMS, dual-layer KMS where required, or client encryption from audit, tenancy, performance, cost, and service integration needs.
Constrain IAM and KMS key policy, grants, service principal, encryption context, region, rotation, disable, deletion, and break-glass ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Transport At Rest Client
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go file-delivery team handles case 1034 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate transport, storage, KMS authorization, context, and rotation evidence; preserve accessible progress, cancellation, status, and recovery evidence; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Transport At Rest Client.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate tls in transit, s3 server-side encryption, kms envelope encryption, and application client-side encryption by threat and feature tradeoff. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Transport At Rest Client, keep Transport At Rest Client connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Key Rotation Revocation Drill
steps.instruction:

Browser code must not import AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, networks, databases, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, vet, fuzz, race, leak, controlled AWS, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.
